note: Technical lens agenda and per-lens records confirmed by the maintainer on 2026-06-19 in the live design workshop. Every selected lens is human-confirmed (lens-question); ui-ux and security-compliance are confirmed-skipped with reasons.

skipped.ui-ux: No application UI. The only user-facing surface is the boundary-stop packet, already owned by the packet contract. Maintainer confirmed n/a.
skipped.security-compliance: No new credentials, auth model, or PII handling. The gate reads existing authorization state and adds no permission surface; hook/shell safety is covered by integration and devops.

agenda: What are the major building blocks and their responsibilities?
Which areas are volatile and should be isolated behind data/interfaces?
What is deliberately out of scope?
Which option best balances simplicity, reversibility, and honest cross-host limits?
architecture-core.decision: Capability-matrix enforce-or-halt built on the EXISTING dormant kind=='gate' dispatcher seat plus Proposal-065 authorization (start-context.json/verdict_history). Harness-free cleaning of the all-host refocus digests. Each host uses its strongest available lever: Claude PreToolUse; Antigravity PreInvocation/Stop; hookless degrades to cooperative instruction plus a mandatory halt. Split guard: stop for a maintainer split/defer verdict if real enforcement requires a broad host-model rewrite rather than a bounded gate-provider + digest + fallback change.

agenda: What responsibilities belong together vs separate?
Which dependencies point inward vs outward?
Reuse or add - and at what abstraction?
component-design.decision: Reuse-heavy: the refocus engine, the dormant gate seat + dispatcher, 065 authorization (Test/Add-SpecrewBoundaryAuthorization), the Claude specrew-gate-stop skill, HandoverStore marker capture, and the per-host host.psd1 manifests. Four focused additions: (1) a host-neutral fallback renderer with verdict-marker emission, (2) a per-host capability declaration in host.psd1, (3) a gate provider on the dormant seat, (4) non-Claude verdict-marker capture. No parallel system.

agenda: What state does enforcement read/write and who owns it?
Is a new persistent store needed?
data-storage.decision: Reuse .specrew/start-context.json (boundary_enforcement + verdict_history) via SessionStateAccessor. The SPECREW-VERDICT-BOUNDARY marker is the verdict-capture mechanism. Per-host capability is a static field in host.psd1. No new persistent store.

agenda: What are the contracts between the cleaned digest, the gate, and verdict capture?
How does each host plug in?
integration-api.decision: Three contracts: (1) a host-appropriate refocus-digest render (harness-free); (2) a gate-provider decision (PreToolUse permissionDecision where available, the host's strongest lever otherwise); (3) the SPECREW-VERDICT-BOUNDARY marker as the host-neutral verdict-capture contract.

observability-resilience.decision: Fail-open where over-blocking would break legitimate work, but halt/fail-closed when a human-judgment boundary genuinely cannot be enforced (the enforce-or-halt tension). Loud WARN reason codes. Bounded evidence; no full prompt/transcript logging.

agenda: How is the new surface deployed and removed?
devops-operations.decision: Deploy via existing mechanisms: deploy-squad-runtime.ps1 host-scoping for the fallback skill, and the refocus/instruction deploy for the cleaned digests. Opt-out is respected. No new deploy surface.

agenda: What are the binding non-functional requirements?
requirements-nfr.decision: The no-silent-advance guarantee (enforce-or-halt) is the core NFR. Honest cross-host degraded modes. Real-host dogfood evidence across the supported host set. The gate must stay cheap (mind the ~920ms PreToolUse spawn cost from F-184).

agenda: Which implementation-craft rules bind this work?
code-implementation.decision: The existing PowerShell baseline craft rules. The gate provider and fallback renderer follow the established dispatcher/provider patterns. Explicit fail-open/closed discipline. No new dependencies. Full rule manifest in implementation-rules.yml.
